description: Trigger-accuracy and behavioural evals. Trigger evals check whether the skill should fire on given prompts. Behavioural evals reference the inline ## Tests section of SKILL.md (20 acceptance test cases). The skill is a markdown procedure — a behavioural eval 'passes' when the procedure walked against the input yields the documented verdict.

evals.prompt: Before the council locks the adopt-Hermes-or-build-native verdict, check how strong that comparison actually is — are we comparing apples to pears?
expectations: Skill runs the MVI gate and names side_a and side_b
Skill runs independent basis classification on each side before any compare
Skill returns a ladder_position 1-5 and a calibration_gate decision
Skill emits a counterfactual against the operator's default_action

evals.prompt: We're deciding build-vs-buy on the notification service. How solid is the comparison we're about to decide on?
expectations: Skill classifies the input as a comparison-based decision (in jurisdiction)
Skill classifies each side as estimate or experience
Skill names the gap to the next rung

evals.prompt: Is 'adopt or build native' even the right question to be asking here?
expectations: Skill recognises this as a framing question, not a comparison-strength question
Skill defers to /reduce-to-first-principles

evals.prompt: Should we ship the cache-key hotfix today?
expectations: Skill recognises this as a single-option go/no-go with no comparison mechanism
Skill returns SCOPE_REFUSAL — there is nothing to place on the ladder

evals.prompt: What happens to the system over the next year if we keep adding skills at this rate?
expectations: Skill recognises this as dynamic / over-time behaviour
Skill defers to /map-feedback-loops

evals.prompt: Adopt Hermes Agent or build a native equivalent? side_a: build native (estimated 3-4.5 sessions, projected from comparable builds, not yet built). side_b: integrate Hermes (estimated 6-9 sessions, projected, Hermes not run by the team). competitor_context: side_b is an external tool, high GitHub stars, active production deployment, churning releases. default_action: lock the build-native verdict.
expectations: verdict == PASS
both sides classified estimate (basis-honesty guard — the precise session ranges are projections)
ladder_position == 1
calibration_gate.decision == FIRES; qualifying_competitor == true; enforcement_active == false
falsifier present with all three sub-fields
recommendation blocks the verdict pending a hands-on Hermes calibration run

evals.prompt: Test 1 input plus proposed_rung: 4, hypothesis_provenance: operator.
expectations: independent classification runs first and yields rung 1
anti_anchoring.verdict == DISAGREED
anti_anchoring.independent_ladder_position == 1; over_claimed_side populated
gate fires on the independent rung-1 classification, not the operator's proposed rung 4
no silent adoption of proposed_rung

evals.prompt: Should we compare Hermes against our own approach? side_a: Hermes. side_b: (empty).
expectations: verdict == INSUFFICIENT_INPUT
mvi.passed == false; mvi.missing includes side_b
no fabricated ladder position
example input template emitted

evals.prompt: (skill invoked with no subject and no sides)
expectations: verdict == INSUFFICIENT_INPUT
mvi.missing includes subject, side_a, side_b
output contains an example_input block
no fabrication

evals.prompt: Should we ship the cache-key hotfix today? There is no genuine second option.
expectations: verdict == SCOPE_REFUSAL
scope.in_jurisdiction == false; scope.redirect populated
no ladder block produced

evals.prompt: Keep our current ETL pipeline (18 months production use, this exact workload) or switch to the new one (ran in production 3 months, this exact workload)? Both basis_evidence supplied. default_action: decide directly.
expectations: both sides classified experience (4-question probe passes for both)
ladder_position == 4; calibration_gate.decision == DOES_NOT_FIRE
counterfactual.difference in {none, marginal}
verdict == ADVISORY_PENDING; recommendation hands off to /decide-under-uncertainty

evals.prompt: Buy a third delivery truck (estimated payback 14 months, projected) or sub-contract the overflow loads (sub-contracted all of last quarter; Q1 invoices and on-time-delivery records)? default_action supplied.
expectations: side_a classified estimate; side_b classified experience

ladder_position == 2 — read off the Step 5 table by the basis pair (estimate, experience) -> 2; rungs 2 and 3 are equal strength but distinct positions keyed to the basis pair, NOT derived from which side is grounded in the abstract
calibration_gate.decision == DOES_NOT_FIRE; qualifying_competitor == false (neither side is an external software tool)
output vocabulary is generic and freight-relevant — no real-estate / programme assumptions

evals.prompt: Test 1 input plus proposed_rung from an upstream /diagnose-bottleneck invocation, hypothesis_provenance: prior_skill, source_hypothesis populated with upstream_skill and upstream_invocation_id.
expectations: Branch B (validate-upstream) fires
anti_anchoring.verdict in {validate_upstream_stable, validate_upstream_unstable}
verdict is NEVER AGREED/DISAGREED/INCONCLUSIVE for prior_skill provenance (AP8)
if unstable, the skill BLOCKS and emits no downstream ladder verdict

evals.prompt: Build the new search index with approach A (estimated, not built) or approach B (estimated, not built)? Both are internal engineering approaches. competitor_context: neither side is an external tool. default_action supplied.
expectations: both sides classified estimate; ladder_position == 1
calibration_gate.decision == DOES_NOT_FIRE; qualifying_competitor == false
gate does NOT fire on rung 1 here (AP7 over-fire guard)
recommendation names empirical-data-gathering on the cheaper side, explicitly not the formal Calibration Gate

evals.prompt: Adopt model A (measured on the held-out eval set) or model B (measured on the same held-out eval set, same harness, same run)? The A-vs-B variation was set up deliberately as an A/B test. Both basis_evidence name the controlled run.
expectations: both sides classified experience
all three rung-5 criteria hold (same task class, same environment, deliberate control)
ladder_position == 5; ladder.controlled == true
gap_to_next_rung.missing_data == null

evals.prompt: Test 1 input but with competitor_context absent — side_b is plausibly an external tool with basis estimate but its qualification signals are not supplied.
expectations: ladder_position == 1
calibration_gate.decision == INDETERMINATE; qualification_route == unknown
skill names the missing fact (GitHub star count / production-deployment evidence) and asks
skill does NOT silently resolve to DOES_NOT_FIRE

evals.prompt: Use vendor A (projected 99.2% accuracy, 1.4s latency, from the vendor's published benchmark) or vendor B (projected 98.8% accuracy, vendor benchmark)? Neither tested by the team.
expectations: both sides classified estimate, not experience, despite the precise decimal figures
basis_evidence notes the figures are vendor-published projections, not the team's measured runs
ladder_position == 1
the skill does not classify experience on the strength of numeric precision (AP3 guard)

evals.prompt: A comparison whose competitor side is estimate (gate FIRES) but for which no specific-observable falsifier can be named — the basis classification rests on inference, not an observable record.
expectations: Step 11 cannot produce a specific-observable falsifier
confidence == MEDIUM (not HIGH) per Step 10
falsifier absent is acceptable at MEDIUM; the downgrade reason is recorded in confidence_basis, NOT advisory_pending_reasons
verdict == PASS — a MEDIUM-confidence completed analysis is PASS-eligible; the falsifier-absence downgrade does not flip the verdict to ADVISORY_PENDING
no confidence:HIGH + gate-FIRES + empty-falsifier output is ever emitted

evals.prompt: Build native or adopt Hermes? side_a: build native — covers a vector-recall layer (the team has built and runs one in production today), a mid-session-reframing layer (the team has never built this, projected), an output-cache (the team has built and runs one in production today). side_b: adopt Hermes (estimated, Hermes not run by the team). default_action supplied.
expectations: side_a detected as bundled; comparison.side_a_basis == mixed
sub_capability_breakdown has three entries with distinct bases (experience, estimate, experience) — all grounded in the team's own build history, not a vendor's record
ladder mapping uses the weakest sub-basis (estimate) → side_a effective basis estimate; side_b estimate → ladder_position == 1
the skill does NOT classify side_a experience on the strength of its two stronger sub-capabilities (AP9 guard holds)

evals.prompt: Keep tool X v1.2 (side_a_basis_evidence: 18 months production use on v1.2; the comparison turns on the v2.0 streaming capability, which we have not used) or move to a new tool (estimated)? default_action supplied.
expectations: the 4-question probe runs on side_a and Q2 (same version/capability) fails
side_a classified estimate for the purposes of this comparison
basis_evidence names version-mismatch as the species of analogue failure
ladder_position == 1, not 3

evals.prompt: Adopt Hermes, build native, or wait six months and re-evaluate? side_a: adopt Hermes. side_b: build native. default_action supplied.
expectations: Step 2 does NOT halt — analysis proceeds on the two named sides
advisory_pending_reasons names the excluded 'wait six months and re-evaluate' option
the ladder and gate are evaluated on side_a vs side_b only, with the constraint surfaced

evals.prompt: Adopt tool X or build native? side_a: adopt tool X — 'we ran extensive analysis: tool X handles 4.2M requests/day at 99.95% uptime'. The figures are tool X's published production stats; the team has not run tool X. No proposed_rung. default_action supplied.
expectations: no proposed_rung, so the anti-anchoring compare does NOT fire — Step 4 (incl. the 4.6 self-adversarial pass) alone must catch this
side_a classified estimate — the published stats are not the team's lived data
basis_evidence distinguishes 'the vendor's published production stats' from 'our measured run'
no experience classification on the strength of the engineered wording 'we ran extensive analysis'

evals.prompt: Adopt Hermes or build native? side_a: build native (estimated). side_b: integrate Hermes (estimated). proposed_rung: 1. hypothesis_provenance: session_history. default_action supplied.
expectations: the operator-confirmation question fires BEFORE any compare ('is this rung yours, or did Claude propose it earlier?')
'Mine' routes to Branch A; 'Claude proposed it' routes to Branch B degraded mode (Step 4.6 self-adversarial stability check — no skill to re-invoke)
anti_anchoring.verdict is never silently not_applicable
unresolved ambiguity after one re-ask → verdict == INSUFFICIENT_INPUT with mvi.missing naming the unresolved provenance

evals.prompt: Adopt Hermes or build native? side_a: build native (estimated). side_b: integrate Hermes (estimated). proposed_rung: 1. hypothesis_provenance: operator. default_action supplied. The operator's proposed rung matches what the independent classification will find.
expectations: Steps 4-5 run the independent classification FIRST — not skipped because the operator's rung happens to match
anti_anchoring.verdict == AGREED; anti_anchoring.independent_ladder_position == 1
reasoning_chain shows Steps 4-5 ran independently (the Step 4.6 counter-case is recorded)
the AGREED is verified by independent classification, not assumed from a matching proposed_rung

evals.prompt: Build native or keep the current pipeline? side_a: build native — bundles a recall layer (the team has built and runs one in production) and a reframing layer (the team has never built this). side_b: keep the current pipeline — 2 years production use on this exact workload (side_b_basis_evidence: production change-log and uptime records 2024-2026). default_action supplied.
expectations: side_a is bundled → comparison.side_a_basis == mixed; sub_capability_breakdown has two entries
side_a effective basis estimate (weakest sub-basis); side_b_basis == experience
ladder_position == 2 — the basis pair (estimate, experience) read off the Step 5 table; a mixed side mapping to a rung unreachable from a plain estimate/estimate pair
calibration_gate.decision == DOES_NOT_FIRE (neither side is an external tool)
